ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "project_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., ongoing, completed) */,
  "project_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is managing the project */,
  "project_status_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the project status was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tasks" (
  "task_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task */,
  "task_name" TEXT /* Name of the task */,
  "task_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the task */,
  "completed" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the task is completed */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the task belongs to */,
  "sequence" INTEGER /* Sequence number indicating the order in which the task should be completed */,
  "task_priority" TEXT /* Priority of the task (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "task_deadline" TEXT /* Deadline for completing the task */,
  "task_completed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the task was completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "predecessor_task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task that must be completed first */,
  "successor_task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task that depends on the completion of the predecessor task */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., finish-to-start, start-to-start, finish-to-finish) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_dependencies_predecessor_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("predecessor_task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_dependencies_successor_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("successor_task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., project manager, team member) */,
  "user_password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "user_position" TEXT /* Position of the user within the organization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each task assignment */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task being assigned */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the task is assigned */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the task was assigned */,
  "completed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user completed the task */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "task_progress" (
  "progress_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each progress update */,
  "task_id" INTEGER /* ID of the task for which progress is being reported */,
  "progress_date" TEXT /* Date of the progress update */,
  "progress_percentage" INTEGER /* Percentage of the task completed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on the progress update */,
  "progress_type" TEXT /* Type of progress update (e.g., percentage complete, notes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("progress_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_task_progress_task_id FOREIGN KEY ("task_id") REFERENCES tasks ("task_id")
)
